title: Executive Order 12675—Establishing the National Space Council

flags.justification: A twelve-member, Cabinet-level coordinating body; Sec. 2(c) explicitly states 'the creation and operation of the Council shall not interfere with existing lines of authority and responsibilities in the departments and agencies.'
Sec. 8 requires an annual report on space policy and strategy.
Multiple related functions (policy review, recommendations, coordination, technology exchange) all coherently serve one purpose: national space policy coordination.
A coordination and advisory body with no binding compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination body for an ongoing government function, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 2(c)'s explicit non-interference clause is protective of existing agency authority, not a removal of oversight.
A coordination body with no third-party compliance regime.
Establishes a significant, durable Cabinet-level space-policy coordination body drawing on nearly every relevant department — a meaningful institutional structure.
Extensive membership drawn from every relevant department, combined with the explicit non-interference clause, is designed to avoid rather than create conflict.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.
